metadata.description: Train styles, people and other subjects at blazing speeds.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/flux-lora-fast-training
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/flux-lora-fast-training queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
FLUX LoRA fine-tuning endpoint.

This endpoint fine-tunes a LoRA model on a dataset of images.
By default the fine-tuning process is configured for preprocessing a subject.
The training will generate both segmentation masks and caption for training.

If the `is_style` flag is set to `True`,
the training a style LoRA, which disables auto-captioning and sengmentation.

To provide your own captions, you can include a text file with the same name as
the image file. For example, if you have an image `photo.jpg`, you can include a
text file `photo.txt` with the caption.

Additionally you can include your own masks for the images. If you have a image
`photo.jpg`, you can include a mask file `photo_mask.jpg`.

is_style.title: Is Style
is_style.description: If True, the training will be for a style. This will deactivate segmentation, captioning and will use trigger word instead. Use the trigger word to specify the style.

data_archive_format.description: The format of the archive. If not specified, the format will be inferred from the URL.
data_archive_format.title: Data Archive Format

images_data_url.description:
URL to zip archive with images. Try to use at least 4 images in general the more the better.

        In addition to images the archive can contain text files with captions. Each text file should have the same name as the image file it corresponds to.
images_data_url.title: Images Data Url

is_input_format_already_preprocessed.description: Specifies whether the input data is already in a processed format. When set to False (default), the system expects raw input where image files and their corresponding caption files share the same name (e.g., 'photo.jpg' and 'photo.txt'). Set to True if your data is already in a preprocessed format.
is_input_format_already_preprocessed.title: Is Input Format Already Preprocessed

create_masks.description: If True segmentation masks will be used in the weight the training loss. For people a face mask is used if possible.
create_masks.title: Create Masks

trigger_word.title: Trigger Word
trigger_word.description:
Trigger word to be used in the captions. If None, a trigger word will not be used.
        If no captions are provide the trigger_word will be used instead of captions. If captions are the trigger word will not be used.

steps.description: Number of steps to train the LoRA on.
steps.title: Steps

diffusers_lora_file.description: URL to the trained diffusers lora weights.

config_file.description: URL to the training configuration file.

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

file_name.title: File Name
file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: The mime type of the file.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: FLUX LoRA training optimized for portrait generation, with bright highlights, excellent prompt following and highly detailed results.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/flux-lora-portrait-trainer
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/flux-lora-portrait-trainer queue.

subject_crop.description: If True, the subject will be cropped from the image.
subject_crop.title: Subject Crop

data_archive_format.description: The format of the archive. If not specified, the format will be inferred from the URL.
data_archive_format.title: Data Archive Format

create_masks.description: If True, masks will be created for the subject.
create_masks.title: Create Masks

trigger_phrase.description:
Trigger phrase to be used in the captions. If None, a trigger word will not be used.
        If no captions are provide the trigger_work will be used instead of captions. If captions are provided, the trigger word will replace the `[trigger]` string in the captions.
trigger_phrase.title: Trigger Phrase

learning_rate.title: Learning Rate
learning_rate.description: Learning rate to use for training.

multiresolution_training.description: If True, multiresolution training will be used.
multiresolution_training.title: Multiresolution Training

images_data_url.description:
URL to zip archive with images of a consistent style. Try to use at least 10 images, although more is better.

        In addition to images the archive can contain text files with captions. Each text file should have the same name as the image file it corresponds to.

        The captions can include a special string `[trigger]`. If a trigger_word is specified, it will replace `[trigger]` in the captions.
images_data_url.title: Images Data Url

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Number of steps to train the LoRA on.

resume_from_checkpoint.description: URL to a checkpoint to resume training from.

image_data_url.description:
URL to zip archive with images of a consistent style. Try to use at least 10 images, although more is better.

    The zip can also contain a text file for each image. The text file should be named:
    ROOT.txt
    For example:
    photo.txt

    This text file can be used to specify the edit instructions for the image pair.

    If no text file is provided, the default_caption will be used.

    If no default_caption is provided, the training will fail.

metadata.description: Train LTX-2.3 22B for custom styles and effects.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/ltx23-video-trainer
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/ltx23-video-trainer queue.

Ltx23VideoTrainerInput.title: LTX23Input
Ltx23VideoTrainerInput.description: Input configuration for LTX-2.3 text-to-video training.

trigger_phrase.title: Trigger Phrase
trigger_phrase.description: A phrase that will trigger the LoRA style. Will be prepended to captions during training.

audio_normalize.title: Audio Normalize
audio_normalize.description: Normalize audio peak amplitude to a consistent level. Recommended for consistent audio levels across the dataset.

learning_rate.description: Learning rate for optimization. Higher values can lead to faster training but may cause overfitting.
learning_rate.title: Learning Rate

debug_dataset.title: Debug Dataset
debug_dataset.description: When enabled, the trainer returns a downloadable archive of your preprocessed training data for manual inspection. Use this to verify that your videos, images, and captions were processed correctly before committing to a full training run.

training_data_url.title: Training Data Url
training_data_url.description:
URL to zip archive with videos or images. Try to use at least 10 files, although more is better.

        **Supported video formats:** .mp4, .mov, .avi, .mkv
        **Supported image formats:** .png, .jpg, .jpeg

        Note: The dataset must contain ONLY videos OR ONLY images - mixed datasets are not supported.

        The archive can also contain text files with captions. Each text file should have the same name as the media file it corresponds to.

rank.title: Rank
rank.description: The rank of the LoRA adaptation. Higher values increase capacity but use more memory.

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: Resolution to use for training. Higher resolutions require more memory.

stg_scale.title: Stg Scale
stg_scale.description: STG (Spatio-Temporal Guidance) scale. 0.0 disables STG. Recommended value is 1.0.

validation.title: Validation
validation.description: A list of validation prompts to use during training. When providing an image, _all_ validation inputs must have an image.

frame_rate.title: Frame Rate
frame_rate.description: Target frames per second for the video.

generate_audio_in_validation.title: Generate Audio In Validation
generate_audio_in_validation.description: Whether to generate audio in validation samples.

first_frame_conditioning_p.title: First Frame Conditioning P
first_frame_conditioning_p.description: Probability of conditioning on the first frame during training. Higher values improve image-to-video performance.

auto_scale_input.title: Auto Scale Input
auto_scale_input.description: If true, videos will be automatically scaled to the target frame count and fps. This option has no effect on image datasets.

validation_number_of_frames.title: Validation Number Of Frames
validation_number_of_frames.description: The number of frames in validation videos.

validation_aspect_ratio.title: Validation Aspect Ratio
validation_aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio to use for validation.

audio_preserve_pitch.title: Audio Preserve Pitch
audio_preserve_pitch.description: When audio duration doesn't match video duration, stretch/compress audio without changing pitch. If disabled, audio is trimmed or padded with silence.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio to use for training.

split_input_duration_threshold.title: Split Input Duration Threshold
split_input_duration_threshold.description: The duration threshold in seconds. If a video is longer than this, it will be split into scenes.

with_audio.title: With Audio
with_audio.description: Enable joint audio-video training. If None (default), automatically detects whether input videos have audio. Set to True to force audio training, or False to disable.

validation_negative_prompt.title: Validation Negative Prompt
validation_negative_prompt.default: worst quality, inconsistent motion, blurry, jittery, distorted
validation_negative_prompt.description: A negative prompt to use for validation.

validation_resolution.title: Validation Resolution
validation_resolution.description: The resolution to use for validation.

split_input_into_scenes.title: Split Input Into Scenes
split_input_into_scenes.description: If true, videos above a certain duration threshold will be split into scenes.

number_of_frames.title: Number Of Frames
number_of_frames.description: Number of frames per training sample. Must satisfy frames % 8 == 1 (e.g., 1, 9, 17, 25, 33, 41, 49, 57, 65, 73, 81, 89, 97).

validation_frame_rate.title: Validation Frame Rate
validation_frame_rate.description: Target frames per second for validation videos.

debug_dataset.description: A downloadable archive containing the preprocessed training data, including decoded videos and audio. Only present when `debug_dataset` is enabled in the input.

config_file.description: Configuration used for setting up inference endpoints.

video.description: The URL to the validation videos, if any.

lora_file.description: URL to the trained LoRA weights (.safetensors).

image_url.title: Image Url
image_url.description: An image to use for image-to-video validation. If provided for one validation, _all_ validation inputs must have an image.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The prompt to use for validation.

file_name.title: File Name
file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: The mime type of the file.

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Fine-tune FLUX.2 [klein] 9B from Black Forest Labs with custom datasets. Create specialized LoRA adaptations for specific editing tasks.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/flux-2-klein-9b-base-trainer/edit
info.description: Train image editing LoRAs for FLUX.2 [klein], BFL's latest image model. FLUX.2 [klein] offers enhanced realism, crisper and more accurate text generation, and native image editing capabilities.

image_data_url.title: Image Data Url
image_data_url.description:
URL to the input data zip archive.

    The zip should contain pairs of images. The images should be named:

    ROOT_start.EXT and ROOT_end.EXT
    For example:
    photo_start.jpg and photo_end.jpg

    The zip can also contain up to four reference image for each image pair. The reference images should be named:
    ROOT_start.EXT, ROOT_start2.EXT, ROOT_start3.EXT, ROOT_start4.EXT, ROOT_end.EXT
    For example:
    photo_start.jpg, photo_start2.jpg, photo_end.jpg

    The zip can also contain a text file for each image pair. The text file should be named:
    ROOT.txt
    For example:
    photo.txt

    This text file can be used to specify the edit instructions for the image pair.

    If no text file is provided, the default_caption will be used.

    If no default_caption is provided, the training will fail.

learning_rate.title: Learning Rate
learning_rate.description: Learning rate applied to trainable parameters.

default_caption.title: Default Caption
default_caption.description: Default caption to use when caption files are missing. If None, missing captions will cause an error.

learning_rate.description: Learning rate for LoRA parameters.
learning_rate.title: Learning Rate

image_data_url.title: Image Data Url
image_data_url.description:
URL to the input data zip archive.

    The zip should contain pairs of images. The images should be named:

    ROOT_start.EXT and ROOT_end.EXT
    For example:
    photo_start.jpg and photo_end.jpg

    The zip can also contain more than one reference image for each image pair. The reference images should be named:
    ROOT_start.EXT, ROOT_start2.EXT, ROOT_start3.EXT, ..., ROOT_end.EXT
    For example:
    photo_start.jpg, photo_start2.jpg, photo_end.jpg

    The Reference Image Count field should be set to the number of reference images.

    The zip can also contain a text file for each image pair. The text file should be named:
    ROOT.txt
    For example:
    photo.txt

    This text file can be used to specify the edit instructions for the image pair.

    If no text file is provided, the default_caption will be used.

    If no default_caption is provided, the training will fail.

metadata.description: Train Ideogram on your photos, your style, your subject, your look, from a small set of reference images to images that feel consistently yours

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/ideogram/custom-models
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/ideogram/custom-models queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Train a custom Ideogram model from a ZIP of training images.

End-to-end flow (mirrors ``phota/create-profile`` and
``recraft/v3/create-style``):

1. Download the caller-provided ZIP archive.
2. Create a new Ideogram dataset scoped to the caller.
3. Forward the archive to ``/datasets/{id}/upload_assets``. Ideogram
accepts a ZIP directly and extracts images + optional caption
sidecars server-side.
4. Kick off training.
5. Poll ``/models/{model_id}`` until the model reaches ``COMPLETED``
or ``ERRORED`` (or until ``_TRAINING_MAX_WAIT`` elapses).

Returns the ``model_id`` and ``custom_model_uri`` of the trained model.

images_data_url.title: Images Data Url
images_data_url.description: URL of a ZIP archive of training images. The archive must contain between 10 and 100 images (JPEG, PNG, or WebP). You may include caption sidecar files (``<stem>.txt``) to guide training -- captions are matched to images by filename stem.

custom_model_uri.title: Custom Model Uri
custom_model_uri.description: The trained model's URI. ``/generate`` resolves this automatically from the ``model_id``, but it is surfaced here for callers that use the ``custom_model_uri`` on the public Ideogram v3 generate endpoint directly.

model_id.title: Model Id
model_id.description: The Ideogram model id. Pass this to /generate.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Train LTX-2.3 22B for video transformation or video-conditioned generation.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/ltx23-v2v-trainer
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/ltx23-v2v-trainer queue.

Ltx23V2vTrainerInput.description: Input configuration for LTX-2.3 video-to-video (IC-LoRA) training.
Ltx23V2vTrainerInput.title: LTX23V2VInput

frame_rate.title: Frame Rate
frame_rate.description: Target frames per second for the video.

number_of_frames.title: Number Of Frames
number_of_frames.description: Number of frames per training sample. Must satisfy frames % 8 == 1 (e.g., 1, 9, 17, 25, 33, 41, 49, 57, 65, 73, 81, 89, 97).

split_input_duration_threshold.title: Split Input Duration Threshold
split_input_duration_threshold.description: The duration threshold in seconds. If a video is longer than this, it will be split into scenes.

first_frame_conditioning_p.title: First Frame Conditioning P
first_frame_conditioning_p.description: Probability of conditioning on the first frame during training. Lower values work better for video-to-video transformation.

rank.title: Rank
rank.description: The rank of the LoRA adaptation. Higher values increase capacity but use more memory.

training_data_url.title: Training Data Url
training_data_url.description:
URL to zip archive with videos or images. Try to use at least 10 files, although more is better.

        **Supported video formats:** .mp4, .mov, .avi, .mkv
        **Supported image formats:** .png, .jpg, .jpeg

        Note: The dataset must contain ONLY videos OR ONLY images - mixed datasets are not supported.

        The archive can also contain text files with captions. Each text file should have the same name as the media file it corresponds to.

validation_frame_rate.title: Validation Frame Rate
validation_frame_rate.description: Target frames per second for validation videos.

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: Resolution to use for training. Higher resolutions require more memory.

validation_negative_prompt.default: worst quality, inconsistent motion, blurry, jittery, distorted
validation_negative_prompt.title: Validation Negative Prompt
validation_negative_prompt.description: A negative prompt to use for validation.

validation_aspect_ratio.title: Validation Aspect Ratio
validation_aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio to use for validation.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio to use for training.

validation.title: Validation
validation.description: A list of validation inputs with prompts and reference videos.

validation_number_of_frames.title: Validation Number Of Frames
validation_number_of_frames.description: The number of frames in validation videos.

auto_scale_input.title: Auto Scale Input
auto_scale_input.description: If true, videos will be automatically scaled to the target frame count and fps. This option has no effect on image datasets.

split_input_into_scenes.title: Split Input Into Scenes
split_input_into_scenes.description: If true, videos above a certain duration threshold will be split into scenes.

validation_resolution.title: Validation Resolution
validation_resolution.description: The resolution to use for validation.

trigger_phrase.title: Trigger Phrase
trigger_phrase.description: A phrase that will trigger the LoRA style. Will be prepended to captions during training.

debug_dataset.title: Debug Dataset
debug_dataset.description: When enabled, the trainer returns a downloadable archive of your preprocessed training data for manual inspection. Use this to verify that your videos, images, and captions were processed correctly before committing to a full training run.

stg_scale.title: Stg Scale
stg_scale.description: STG (Spatio-Temporal Guidance) scale. 0.0 disables STG. Recommended value is 1.0.

learning_rate.description: Learning rate for optimization. Higher values can lead to faster training but may cause overfitting.
learning_rate.title: Learning Rate

lora_file.description: URL to the trained IC-LoRA weights (.safetensors).

config_file.description: Configuration used for setting up inference endpoints.

debug_dataset.description: A downloadable archive containing the preprocessed training data, including decoded videos. Only present when `debug_dataset` is enabled in the input.

video.description: The URL to the validation videos (with reference videos side-by-side), if any.

reference_video_url.title: Reference Video Url
reference_video_url.description: URL to reference video for IC-LoRA validation. This is the input video that will be transformed.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The prompt to use for validation.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: The mime type of the file.

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

file_name.title: File Name
file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Train LTX-2 for video transformation or video-conditioned generation.
Train custom LoRAs for personalization, styles or other use cases on top of Ideogram V4.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for ideogram/v4/trainer
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the ideogram/v4/trainer queue.

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: Training image size. Use auto to pick the largest common no-upscale crop from the dataset. Images are center-cropped without upscaling. Available presets: square 1024x1024, landscape 1536x1024, portrait 1024x1536, widescreen 1920x1088, ultrawide 2048x768, phone_wallpaper 1024x1792, social_banner 1584x400. You can also pass a custom WIDTHxHEIGHT string when both values are divisible by 16.

images_data_url.title: Images Data Url
images_data_url.description: URL to a zip archive with training images and captions. The trainer uses the target-only dataset prepared by prepare_dataset_dir. Captions are read from same-stem .txt files.

default_caption.title: Default Caption
default_caption.description: Caption to use when an image has no caption file. If force_default_caption is true, this caption replaces all caption files.

diffusers_lora_file.description: URL to the trained Ideogram V4 LoRA weights.

config_file.description: URL to the public training configuration.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
